title: Layer-by-Layer Assembly of Nanosheet Heterostructures
summary: Development of synthetic strategies for the exfoliation and controlled assembly of inorganic nanosheets into hybrid thin-film structures.
detailedDescription: This project aims to study the basic chemical physics of 2D exfoliation. We are synthesizing new parent layered materials and using soft-chemical routes to produce stable colloid suspensions of single-layer sheets. We are developing layer-by-layer deposition techniques to build artificial superlattices with tailored magnetic and optical properties.

title: New Photocatalytic Systems for Solar Energy Conversion
summary: Designing high-surface-area transition metal oxide materials optimized for hydrogen generation and solar light harvesting.
detailedDescription: This research investigates how structural tuning and doping of metal oxides influences charge-carrier lifetime and catalytic efficiency. We are integrating exfoliated sheets into catalyst platforms to maximize accessible surface area and active catalytic sites.
